summary: Manage IAM roles, managed-policy attachments, and access keys. Tagging is self-service; policy attach/detach and access-key status toggles (rotation) are guardrailed with a second approver; role creation and deletion are security-relevant and architectural, so they route to an engineer.

operations.title: Add a tag to an IAM role
operations.summary: Add a tag to a role. Metadata only — no change to its permissions or trust policy.
operations.description: Add a single new key/value tag to an existing role's tags map. Metadata-only — no change to permissions, trust policy, or attached policies.

params.name: role
params.label: Role
params.help: The existing IAM role to tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed role can be targeted; adding a tag is metadata-only and never changes the role's permissions or trust policy.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: New tag key, up to 128 chars; must not already exist on the role.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value, up to 256 characters.

operations.title: Attach a managed policy to a role
operations.summary: Attach a pre-approved read-only policy to a role, expanding what it can access.
operations.description: Attach a pre-approved AWS-managed policy to an existing role via a new policy attachment. Limited to read-only/support policies from the allowlist; anything write-capable or administrative requires an engineer-authored change.

params.name: role
params.label: Role
params.help: The existing IAM role that will receive the new policy attachment, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed role can be targeted; the policies it can gain here are limited to the pre-approved read-only/support allowlist.

params.name: policy_arn
params.label: Managed policy
params.help: Pre-approved read-only/support policies only. Write-capable or administrative policies must go through an engineer-authored change.

operations.title: Detach a managed policy from a role
operations.summary: Detach a policy from a role, revoking those permissions immediately for anything using the role. Reversible — it can be reattached.
operations.description: Remove an existing policy attachment from a role, revoking the permissions it granted. Reversible — the same policy can be reattached — but takes effect immediately for anything assuming the role.

params.name: role
params.label: Role
params.help: The existing IAM role to detach the policy from, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed role can be targeted; detaching revokes that policy's permissions immediately for anything assuming the role, but the same policy can be reattached.

params.name: policy_arn
params.label: Managed policy to detach
params.help: Must currently be attached to the selected role.

operations.title: Deactivate or reactivate an access key
operations.summary: Turn an access key off or back on — the first step in rotating a compromised key. Turning it off breaks anything still using it right away.
operations.description: Toggle an access key's status. Set to Inactive to immediately disable a suspected-compromised or superseded key without deleting it — the standard first step of key rotation; set to Active to restore a previously deactivated key. The key material itself is never handled here.

params.name: access_key
params.label: Access key
params.help: The existing IAM access key whose status you are toggling, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed key can be targeted; only its Active/Inactive status changes and the secret key material is never displayed or handled here.

params.name: new_status
params.label: New status
params.help: Inactive disables the key immediately; Active re-enables it. Any application still using a key you set Inactive will fail authentication right away.

operations.title: Create a new IAM role
operations.summary: Request a brand-new role; an engineer designs its trust and permissions and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a brand-new IAM role with a trust (assume-role) policy and initial permissions. Trust boundary and permission scope are security-relevant design decisions, so an engineer authors and reviews the change.

params.name: proposed_name
params.label: Proposed role name
params.help: Lower-case, hyphenated; the engineer will confirm the final name and path against the estate's naming convention.

params.name: purpose
params.label: Purpose / assuming principal
params.help: Describe the workload, service, or principal that will assume this role — the engineer designs the trust and permission policies from this.

decisions: Trust (assume-role) policy — which principals may assume this role

operations.title: Delete an IAM role
operations.summary: Permanently delete a role. This can't be undone, and anything using the role loses access immediately; an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Destroy an IAM role, detaching all its policies and invalidating its trust relationship. Irreversible — anything currently assuming the role loses access immediately. High blast radius; an engineer authors and reviews the change.

params.name: role
params.label: Role
params.help: The existing IAM role to destroy, chosen from the estate inventory so only a real, Terraform-managed role can be targeted; deletion is irreversible and anything still assuming the role loses access immediately, which is why an engineer authors and reviews this change.

operations.title: Set forced policy detach for an IAM role
operations.summary: Turn on a setting that lets a role be deleted even while policies are still attached, removing a safety guardrail. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Set the role's `force_detach_policies` flag (true/false). When true, Terraform force-detaches any attached managed policies before destroying the role; enabling it removes the guardrail that otherwise blocks destroying a role that still has policies attached, so an engineer authors and reviews the change.

params.name: role
params.label: Role
params.help: The IAM role to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: force_detach_policies
params.label: Force detach policies
params.help: true = Terraform force-detaches attached policies before destroying the role; false (AWS default) = destroy is blocked while policies remain attached.

operations.title: Rename an IAM user
operations.summary: Rename a user. Credentials keep working, but anything referencing the old name can break silently.
operations.description: Change the `name` of an existing IAM user in place via Terraform's `name` argument — applied through the IAM UpdateUser API, not a destroy/recreate (the user keeps its unique ID and credentials). Its ARN changes, so external resource-based policies or trust relationships that reference this user by name or ARN can silently break. Note: per the AWS provider docs, if managed policies are attached to the user via the legacy `aws_iam_policy_attachment` resource, the rename fails with a `DeleteConflict` error unless `force_destroy` is set first. Value is bounded to the documented IAM user-name character set.

params.name: user_name
params.label: User Name
params.help: The IAM user to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: new_name
params.label: New User Name
params.help: Upper/lowercase alphanumerics with no spaces, plus =,.@-_ ; 1-64 characters. Names are case-insensitive and must be unique in the account. Renaming changes the user's ARN, which can break external policies that reference it by name.

operations.title: Set forced destroy for an IAM user
operations.summary: Turn on a setting that lets a user be deleted even with keys, a password, or MFA set up, removing a safety guardrail. Engineer-reviewed.
operations.description: Set the Terraform `force_destroy` argument on an existing IAM user. When true, a later destroy of this user succeeds even if it still has non-Terraform-managed access keys, a login profile, or MFA devices; false (the default) keeps that guardrail in place. Enabling it removes a deletion safety guardrail, so an engineer authors and reviews the change. Applied in-place — the flag itself changes nothing about the live user until a destroy is attempted.

params.name: user_name
params.label: User Name
params.help: The IAM user to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: force_destroy
params.label: Force destroy
params.help: true = allow a future destroy of this user even if it has non-Terraform-managed access keys, a login profile, or MFA devices; false = keep the default guardrail that blocks such a destroy.

operations.title: Rename an IAM group
operations.summary: Rename a group. Members and access stay the same, but anything referencing the old name can break.
operations.description: Change the name of an existing IAM group in-place (aws_iam_group.name). Attached policies and group memberships are preserved, but the group's ARN changes together with its name, which can silently break external references (resource-based policies, automation, or documentation) that identify the group by its old name or ARN. Blast radius scales with membership size, so it is guardrailed; no permissions are directly changed.

params.name: group
params.label: Group
params.help: The IAM group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: new_name
params.label: New group name
params.help: Upper/lowercase alphanumerics plus = , . @ _ - and no spaces; 1-128 characters (AWS IAM group-name limit). Names are case-insensitive and must be unique within the account.

operations.title: Update IAM group path
operations.summary: Move a group to a different organizational folder path. Organizational metadata only — no change to its permissions or members.
operations.description: Change the organizational path of an existing IAM group in-place (aws_iam_group.path), e.g. from / to /division_abc/. Path is hierarchical metadata used for organization and IAM path-based policy scoping; it does not change the group's permissions or membership. In-place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: group
params.label: Group
params.help: The IAM group to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: path
params.label: New path
params.help: Hierarchical path; must begin with '/'. Alphanumerics, '/', '-', '_'; up to 512 characters (AWS IAM path limit). Defaults to '/'.

operations.title: Rename inline role policy
operations.summary: Rename an inline policy. Applying REPLACES it — the permission grant briefly drops during the change, though what it grants stays the same.
operations.description: Change the name of an existing inline role policy (the aws_iam_role_policy `name` argument). The policy document and the permissions it grants are unchanged. Important: `name` is ForceNew in the AWS provider (confirmed from provider source; the internal reference doc had recorded it as in-place), so Terraform replaces the inline policy (destroy + recreate) rather than editing it in place, briefly interrupting the grant during apply.

params.name: role_policy
params.label: Inline role policy
params.help: Select the existing inline role policy (aws_iam_role_policy) to rename.

params.name: new_name
params.label: New policy name
params.help: New IAM policy name: letters, digits, and _ + = , . @ - only; 1-128 characters (validRolePolicyName charset). Changing the name forces Terraform to replace the inline policy (destroy + recreate).

operations.title: Move inline policy to a different role
operations.summary: Move an inline policy to a different role. Applying REPLACES it — access briefly drops on the old role before it's granted on the new one.
operations.description: Reassign an existing inline policy to a different IAM role (the aws_iam_role_policy `role` argument), moving the permission grant from one principal to another. `role` is ForceNew in the AWS provider (confirmed from provider source; the internal reference doc had recorded it as in-place), so Terraform destroys the inline policy on the current role and recreates it on the destination role. Security-relevant boundary change, so an engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: role_policy
params.label: Inline role policy to move
params.help: Select the existing inline role policy (aws_iam_role_policy) to move.

params.name: new_role
params.label: Destination IAM role
params.help: Destination IAM role to reattach the inline policy to. The policy is destroyed on the current role and recreated on this one (role is ForceNew).
